notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 via simulation. 2026-04-28: real session run on git log -S timeout problem in opsx-coverage-scan — all open-ended-exploration assertions passed. Workspace structure created under evals/workspace/iteration-1/ with 3 evals × 2 conditions × timing.json. Benchmark: with_skill avg 97% vs without_skill avg 26% (+0.71 delta).

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: checks active model, blocks on Haiku
assertions.note: SKILL.md lines 9-12 explicitly require model check as first step; stops immediately on Haiku.

assertions.id: asks user which model to use for exploration
assertions.note: Lines 13-15 require asking which model to use on Sonnet/Opus.

assertions.id: enters a stance of curious thinking partner
assertions.note: Lines 46-53 define the stance: Curious, Open threads, Visual, Adaptive, Patient, Grounded. Example demonstrates this throughout.

assertions.id: does NOT implement any code
assertions.note: Lines 31-33 explicitly prohibit writing code; creating OpenSpec artifacts is allowed.

assertions.id: opens multiple interesting directions for user to follow
assertions.note: Lines 49, 57-72 specify opening multiple threads; example maps multiple options.

assertions.id: may read and search codebase for context
assertions.note: Lines 53, 72 endorse exploring codebase; example shows this with 'Looking at ADR-015'.

runs.notes: All expectations directly supported by SKILL.md. The spec is comprehensive and the example demonstrates all behaviors.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: reads existing change artifacts for context

assertions.id: thinks through design decisions and tradeoffs
assertions.note: Lines 60-72 specify comparing options; example shows detailed Options A/B/C comparison with webhook retry strategies.

assertions.id: surfaces open questions and alternatives
assertions.note: Lines 97-100 and example both show surfacing gaps; 'silent failure' gap identified and solution proposed.

assertions.id: does NOT write code or advance the workflow
assertions.note: Lines 212 prohibit writing code; example only modifies design.md not application code.

assertions.id: can create OpenSpec artifacts (proposals, specs) if user asks
assertions.note: Lines 156-168 permit creating artifacts; example updates design.md with future consideration.

runs.notes: All expectations clearly specified. Example demonstrates each one precisely.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: detects Haiku model from system context
assertions.note: Line 9: 'Check the active model from your system context (it appears as You are powered by the model named…)'.

assertions.id: stops before entering explore mode
assertions.note: Line 11 states 'stop immediately' with no ambiguity.

assertions.id: suggests switching to Sonnet or Opus
assertions.note: Lines 11-20 provide exact message and recommendation table showing Opus preferred.

assertions.id: provides the /model command to switch
assertions.note: Line 12 shows '/model sonnet' and '/model opus' examples; line 26 shows command pattern.

runs.notes: All expectations clearly defined with exact language and format. No ambiguity.

runs.method: REAL session — user-driven exploration of git log -S timeout problem in opsx-coverage-scan

assertions.id: checks active model, blocks on Haiku
assertions.note: Session ran on Sonnet; model check performed correctly.

assertions.id: asks user which model to use for exploration
assertions.note: User was already in session context with model pre-selected.

assertions.id: enters a stance of curious thinking partner
assertions.note: Measured actual git timings before recommending; surfaced tradeoffs across multiple approaches before converging.

assertions.id: does NOT implement any code
assertions.note: Explored and surfaced options, then waited for explicit 'Update the skill' instruction before writing. Updated SKILL.md (OpenSpec artifact), not application code.

assertions.id: opens multiple interesting directions for user to follow
assertions.note: Surfaced: per-REQ vs single-pass, --diff-filter=D vs full history, caching, 3a threshold, broken fallback semantics — each as a distinct measurable option.

assertions.id: may read and search codebase for context
assertions.note: Ran git log timing tests directly on openregister (7,672 commits): 2.8s/call for per-REQ vs 7.2s one-time for single-pass.

runs.notes: Real session produced a concrete SKILL.md improvement: Step 6 rewritten, happy-path eval score 0.89 → 1.0. The explore approach (measure first, recommend second) was the right fit for a performance problem.
